entry.title: Black Ops 3 - Zombies v0.2.5
entry.content:
**Apworld:** - Fire and Void bow checks are disabled by default. They will be default enabled after restoration is working well for them. - Starting points bonus default increased from 2500 to 3000 - Map Specific items turned off by default again until there is better UI for them
**Mod:** - Basic Gobblegums are now unlocked to use in gum loadouts - Der Eisendrache Bows: - There is now a basic warning on-screen when bows are being restored - Storm and Wolf bows restore up to the arrow being placed on the pedestals. Until it can be worked around enjoy the free max ammo - Void bow restores up to needing to be fed 6 crawlers - Fix Storm and Wolf bow restoration not happening if you are wielding a Fire or Void bow - Fix rocket scene not running when restoring during the channelling stone step - Fix MPD not spawning when beating the keeper boss if restoring a save after the channelling stone step - Prevent player data saving when in beast mode - Fix crash when going down related to stamin-up and widow's wine
Wanted workshop map is still broken for now.

entry.title: Black Ops 3 - Zombies v0.3.0
entry.content: - Improved logic for better map progression by @PIEisFANTASTIC - Starting Quick Revive is now an option - Starting Map Hints is now an option (default on) - Archipelago Shop: - Checkpoints can now be made by spending a Checkpoint Token at the Shop - Starting Checkpoint Tokens added to starting inventory is configurable in yaml (default 1) - Additional Checkpoint Tokens shuffled into the item pool available as AP items are configurable in yaml (default 1) - Shadows of Evil: - If all 4 worms are collected, they will be automatically placed on their basins when restoring - Zetsubou No Shima: - Seed count will now save and restore - Fix 2 perk machines sometimes not being on the map when restoring after round 6 - Revelations: - Writing on the Wall side ee is now a check - Helmet of Siegfried is now a check - Keeper Protector parts will now save and restore - All masks will now save and restore, including equip state - Individual progress flags for masks are saved individually - Helmet of Siegfried will take 20 seconds to restore - Number of masks counting as checks can be randomized (default 3) - Like elemental bows, masks without checks will not show in the Checks tab in the main menu - Mask checks now describe the requirements in the check name - **Almost all the online guides have errors** - Some Masks are now multiple checks to reward longer quests - 2 checks: Helmet of the King, Keeper Skull and Margwa Head - 3 checks: Apothicon God mask - (Castle) has been renamed to (Der Eisendrache) in all instances - Prevent wearable 'perk' being saved as a real perk on Revelations - Prevent gobblegum and perk bottles weapons being saved to loadout

entry.title: Schedule I v3.5.3
entry.content: Updated logic for weed and cocaine recipes not requiring Warehouse access.

entry.title: Manual_Meteos_Konoko v1.1
entry.content: Goal is now get 2 planets, one of them being Meteo instead of needing all planets

entry.title: Monster Hunter World v0.1.3a0
entry.content:
Note: This patch will work with older games. You can run the generator or just replace the .dll in NativePC to fix the master rank goal completion. However, any changes to logic will not be applied unless games are regenerated with new apworld.
**FIXES** - Fixed an unlikely bug that would cause the game to crash every time the player loaded into a quest. - No longer will players receive a useless extra weapon and armor upgrade. - Simple fix were players would not receive items if they started a fresh save and had items waiting to be sent. Still a very specific edge case can cause this to happen but will be fixed in the next major release. - Removed the redundant Flying Sparks quest from the location pool. - Rathalos Rematch, The Fires of Hell Bite Deep, A Light Upon the River’s Gloom, and Today's Special: Hunter Flambé added to quest locations. No longer will they feel left out. - The Wings of Wrath Bite Deep location now has the correct required quest. - Gold and Astral tickets having the wrong ID thus not being given correctly. - Fixed typo in Antidote.

entry.title: Manual_ResidentEvil4_VincentsSin v2.0.9
entry.content: - Fixed naming mismatch for `Progressive Attaché Case` in `items.json`

entry.title: Widget Inc v0.2.0
entry.content: - [Client] Hinted items in the tech tree glow a different color if hinted - Cyan: Hinted & In Logic - Purple: Hinted & Not In Logic - [Client] Location name in tech tree's font size increased 8 -> 12

entry.title: Mega Man Zero 3 v0.2.1
entry.content:
EX Skills and Suit Chips have been implemented!
# Changes
- Body and Leg chips have been implemented. - EX Skills have been implemented. - These are awarded based on your score on the level you just completed, rather than on your average score going into that level (Yes that's how they worked in the vanilla game). - Cyber Elves that boost your ranking for one level will also work in awarding the EX Skill location checks. Just use them before killing the boss. - The option that forces your rank to be "S" has been replaced with an option that will always reward you with an EX Skill regardless of your rank. This option is recommended, as currently, EX Skills are missable. In a later update you will be able to revisit levels and re-attempt an A rank.
# Known Bugs - EX Skills are currently missable. If you do not receive an A or higher on a level with an EX Skill check, there is no way to revisit it without loading an earlier save or savestate. This will be fixed in a later update. Consider using the "Always reward EX-Skill" option.

entry.title: Sonic Adventure DX v1.2.1rc0.post0
entry.content:
## [v1.2.1 pre-release 1] - 2026-03-24
### Added:
- You can now start with the Egg Carrier transformed (new default) or untransformed. - Added mod settings to disable the Ring Link sounds. - The map button can be reassigned or disabled in the mod settings. - You can also disable the in-game tracker while viewing the map (hidden by default). - Setting to ignore traps and filler items when connecting, enabled by default. - In key-item gating mode, closed doors will show a lock icon instead of the "no entry" symbol. - Updated setup guide to include Linux/Steam Deck information. - When entering a level with random level entrances, the alternative entrances to that level will be revealed on the map. - This applies to Speed Highway, Lost World, Final Egg and Sky Deck. - When playing with Random Level Entrances, a question mark icon will be shown in-game as well. - The icon will change to the level's initials after visiting the entrance for the first time.
### Changed:
- Map now shows the transformed state of the Egg Carrier. - Improved logic for the Egg Carrier transformation. - Redrawn map to be clearer and more consistent. - Improved contrast between icons and the map background. - Changed Chao Garden icons to be more distinguishable from level entrances. - Several logic updates to Expert DC and Expert DX.
### Fixed:
- Fixed typo in mission blacklist option. - Fixed typo in capsule sanity description. - Removed Big's upgrade from the Arsenal. - Lines and dots for the map now scale properly with the resolution. - Removed Angel Island to MRMain logic skips. - Fish tracker will no longer be shown for Amy and Gamma. - Fixed mission 51 card not being in the correct position. - Fixed "new" icon not showing up in the map. - Alternative entrances to stages are disabled when Random Level Entrances is off. - Fixed disabled capsules still being tracked by the arrow. - Fixed missing Life Belt upgrade for Big in the Ice Cave. - Fixed double ladder for Amy in Ice Cave. - Fixed Sky Chase 2 entrance transforming the Egg Carrier when exiting it.

entry.title: Stardew Valley v7.4.4
entry.content: - Introduced hotkey (config file, defaults to N) to open mail remotely [@itepastra] - Improved the community - Added a reroll button on Help Wanted quests, similar to the one on Special Orders - Reorganized the character creation menu to make the connection text fields bigger, and hopefully, less prone to FaQs

entry.title: Casualties: Unknown v0.7.2
entry.content:
### Casualties: Unknown Archipelago v0.7.2 This update is compatible with worlds generated on version 0.7.0 and above!
For instructions on how to set up and play this mod, check the [README](https://github.com/Nichologeam/CUAP/blob/master/README.md).
# Additions - New UI that shows how many Archipelago Items are left to collect. # Changes - Ceiling Rye and Skullcrushers now drop Archipelago Items when broken. - Archipelago Items now show the owning player's name and game in their name. - Increased the size of the Layer Selector. - The Skillsanity Tracker is now hidden while Unchipped mode is active. (will probably make this a YAML option later) # Fixes - Earning extra skill experience at Skillsanity level 14 should no longer cause an IndexOutOfRange error. - The game now pauses while selecting the next layer, instead of silently running in the background.
# Known Issues - Getting two traps in quick succession can cause the incorrect slot name inside the trap Moodle - Universal Tracker shows the wrong Moodles being in logic when Moodlesanity is set to Questboard - A NullReferenceException is sometimes thrown when the Layer Selector opens. This seems to be an issue with how Harmony interacts with Prefix patches on IEnumerators. - If more than one Archipelago Item exists, they will all share the same sprite and description. This is a limitation of the original game, and will not be fixed.
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Nichologeam/CUAP/compare/v0.7.1...v0.7.2

entry.title: CrossCode v0.8.2rc1
entry.content:
## Changes to Generation
* Replaced the check **Vagabond Dojo Key** with **Trials of the Golden Bugs** (@CodeTriangle). * This change turns the check that gives the Dojo Key in vanilla into a quest reward so that the item hidden behind it can be previewed. * This new location has the same ID as the previous one, so this change only affects where in the cutscene the item is acquired.
## Changes to Mod
* Applied a series of changes to the Archipelago menus to make creating and playing games more intuitive (@CodeTriangle): - Allow any NG+ options to be selected regardless of previous save file completion. - No longer load into a game without NG+ enabled when exiting the text client menu using cancel button. - Add a clearer indication that NG+ options can be edited from the New Game Plus menu found in the Archipelago menu. - Block out "carry over" section of NG+ when editing options to indicate that they cannot be modified after save file creation. - Stop the game from blocking infinitely when entering new login information after previously entering login information and disconnecting.
## Migration Guide
This version of the mod and apworld **should be safe** to upgrade to from 0.8.1, 0.8.0, or 0.7.x. There is **some slight risk** involved in both of the changes made. You should not be in any serious danger. * There is a slight chance that some interactions break due to the generation change (but this can be fixed easily). * There is a small chance that a user interface may becomes unresponsive in a strange situation.

entry.title: Tevi v1.4.15
entry.content:
## Change log ### All + Valhalla Breath West - Money Room X: 9 Y: 1 -> missing Crossbomb + Extended + Overworld teleporter are now useable in a Memine race when teleporter mode is active
### AP + Added Mananite- and Magitite-Amount options for Archipelago + Fixed upgrade able item count

entry.title: Flipwitch Forbidden Sex Hex v1.0.6
entry.content:
New Feature: - Most H scenes are skipped save game over scenes. Includes gachapon. - A little buggy. Just button mash through the times you hear dialogue move but you can't move. It'll get there.
Changes: - Instead of relying on the index as a cutoff point, items are checked via a dictionary which checks if the item was received and if not send it. This should hopefully resolve the problem where dying a lot makes you "skip" something. - Receiving items seemed to work on my end, but needs further testing. - Instead of path order determining damage, its based on HP items received. This should help make it so you don't feel like a late area will kill you instantly due to no HP upgrade items sent. - Removed the progression label from HP upgrades, since its not considered for logic anymore.
Quick Hotfix: - I added a warning if the previous item wasn't sent but it wasn't very verbose and called itself if the index was 0, which would probably confuse someone and make people offer bad reports.

entry.title: Flipwitch Forbidden Sex Hex v1.0.7
entry.content: Bugfix: - Events for MonoBot and Bunny Club NPCs had no logic (not the actual locations, but events if Quests were on)

entry.title: Borderlands 2 v0.5.1
entry.content: - updated jump heights - new ramps to make areas navigable without jump: arid nexus boneyard and gluttony gulch - buncha logic fixes, many around prerequisite missions, moving challenges to later - remove leviathan check for now - add enemy checks: FaceMcShooty and Jacques O'Lantern - travel chat commands are now case/symbol insensitive

entry.title: Glover v1.0.1
entry.content:
Changelog: - UT Support by [Ars-Ignis](https://github.com/Ars-Ignis) - Minor changes to support Poptracker's Exporting with [AP Logic Builder](https://github.com/Smg065/GloverAPLogicBuilder/releases/tag/1.0.0) - Minor changes to support [RunInAWeek's Poptracker](https://github.com/RunInAWeek/Glover-Ap-Tracker/releases) - Experimental change to Checkpoints in Crn3 in an attempt to solve checkpoint bugs.
Fixes: - More trap compatibility in TrapLink - Added the Lighthouse Life on Hard Logic - Option errors are no longer declared but not raised when entering scores. - Dribble for Starting Life in Prehistoric Bonus - Stairs flagged as needed for Training Goal - Chest Access and Chest Life in Prt1 work as expected - Prt3 Trampoline Lobster is no longer an open check - Trap/Filler Percentage being set to 0 no longer bypasses trap/filler weighting - Deathlink sends a message - Deathlink packages are no longer eaten when TrapLink is expecting a death trap - Links send the context when override is toggled - Prt2: Switch Garibs are considered part of the Switch Platform instead of poolside - Otw3's end w/ball requires your final walkway access instead of your lights platform access. - Bonus Level All Garib checks work as expected - Fixed Lower Monolith overflowing when getting more than 4 items - Custom Mr. Tip text when playing with The Grinch AP
VERY big thanks to the Glover community for finding and fixing many of these issues, along with adding all the nice tools and features outside of the direct .apworld implementation.
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Smg065/GloverArchipelago/commits/v1.0.1

entry.title: PokePark v1.2.0
entry.content:
## What's Changed * fixed Dolphin Cheat Incompatibility by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/76 * improved deathlink to support Overworld and Attraction by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/78 * added Ending trigger on goal completion by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/79 * displaying client messages ingame by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/81 * updated ap client to support new patcher system by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/94 * 95 pokepark colorized in game client messages by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/99 * fixed beach zone spearow flag by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/100 * increased requirements for Arcanine, Jolteon and Flygon by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/101 * 85 pokepark power randomize option customizable by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/102 * 103 pokepark fps enhancement by @Mekurushi in https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/pull/104
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mekurushi/Archipelago_Pokepark/compare/1.1.3...1.2.0

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content:
As this is a prerelease, **there may be several bugs that could take from the experience or result in the creation of an unbeatable seed**, This release should only be used for those who want to test out the new features or want to help find bugs ~~or those who dont care and just want to play anyways~~
Honestly, I think I found most of the bugs. Any remaining unknown bugs are likely located in the client or mod, which means that a hotfix should be able to fix them without the need to generate a new game. That being said, I cannot guarantee this is the case, so don't say I didn't warn you.
## Whats New - Completely redone Mod and APWorld code - Added Delve access items and completion locations - New Goal: Dungeon Delver, complete every delve included in your seed - changed the wayshrine checks to activate when interacting with the wayshrine, not just first discovery. This is for those who want to use an existing character for the AP run - Added new system to prevent players from interacting with interactables that lead to locked zones - Added new system to block traveling to other zones via dialogue with an NPC - Added new system to limit how much gold the player can have on them - New Options - Delves_per_region: How many delves to include from each zone, goes up to 18, set to 0 to disable zone locations and locking - Enable Main Quests: to enable and disable locations for the main quest, must be enabled for the main quest goal or if coldharbour is your goal for zone quest goal - enable Wayshine checks: to enable and disable checks for interacting with wayshrines - base gold cap: what you want the gold cap to be without any wallet increase items, set to 0 to enable gold limiting - More that I can't remember off the top of my head
## Known Bugs: - Leaving the client on for an extended period of time can result in the client wiping all items sent to the mod, meaning the game may tell you that you don't have something you should have. If this occurs, you should just be able to close and reopen the client, and upon reconnection, it should fix all items sent to the game. Ill be looking into what causes this but its likely a 0.4.0+ issue to fix for when I rewrite the client like I did with the apworld and mod in this one - Setting your goal region to random can cause it to pick a region inaccessible with a low zone count, causing generation errors. This error occurs at the beginning of generation, so you should know if there's a problem pretty quick. You can avoid this by manually choosing a goal zone or trying to regen so it picks a different zone until you get it to work. Note that the lower you set the zone count, the more likely this is to occur. I will be looking into fixing this before 0.3.0 proper. - Controller can break on reloads, Ill be looking into this fairly soon. Can be worked around by reloadUI until controller works

entry.title: Total War Warhammer 3 v0.9.0.post2
entry.content:
REQUIRES 0.6.7-rc1 BETA BUILD OF ARCHIPELAGO OR BUILD FROM SOURCE https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7-rc1
Added more traps. Added blacklist for traps that you don't want. Fixed crabs not working Corrected default yaml settings.
Main settlement buildings are no longer locked
Only for conquest mode at the moment: Added "Sanity" and "Ritualsanity" locations, enable in the yaml (not all factions implemented for ritualsanity at this time). Deathlink should no longer break games when played over multiple sessions.
Ritualsanity factions: High Elves, Dark Elves, Empire, Skaven, Tomb Kings, Greenskins (They only have one ritual), Beastmen Some rituals locations might not trigger when unlocked, depending on if the game actually treats them as rituals, let me know on discord if a ritual location does not send.
Installation instructions can be found [here](https://github.com/jordansds/Archipelago_TWW3_Alt/blob/main/README.md).
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jordansds/Archipelago_TWW3_Alt/compare/v0.8.8...v0.8.9

entry.title: Ratchet & Clank 2 v0.6.4.2
entry.content:
Testing Dynamic PINE ports for multi PCSX2 instance support. Also adds auto connection functionally to the client using patch data. Based on: https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/releases/tag/v0.6.4 v0.6.4.2: Now also has UT integrated, and yaml-less support. Works as long as UT is installed. Also fixed some first person logic issues. Planned: More UT features. "Glitch" logic. Internal or external map. Auto map tab switching. Community suggested logic changes.
### Setup You need every RaC2 option in your `host.yaml` setup. That means: `iso_file` to a valid vanilla iso in your Archipelago directory (should be already from normal setup) `iso_start` to your PCSX2 exe (Make sure to use `/`) `game_ini` to a game settings file in your PCSX2's gamesettings folder. (Usually `%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini`)
Example: ```yaml rac2_options: # File name of the Ratchet & Clank 2 ISO iso_file: "Ratchet & Clank - Going Commando (USA) (v1.01).iso" # Set this false to never autostart an iso (such as after patching), # Set it to true to have the operating system default program open the iso # Alternatively, set it to a path to a program to open the .iso file with (like PCSX2) iso_start: "C:/Emulators/(PS2) PCSX2/pcsx2-qt.exe" # Set to file path to an existing PCSX2 game setting INI file to have the patcher # create an appropriately named INI with the rest of the patch output. This can be used to # allow you to use you own custom PCSX2 setting with patched ISO. game_ini: "%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini" ```
Game settings ini example: ```ini [EmuCore] EnablePINE = true PINESlot = 28011
[Achievements] Enabled = false
[MemoryCards] Slot1_Enable = true Slot2_Enable = false Slot1_Filename = APgames.ps2 ``` Recommended you use your settings for vanilla RaC2. But you can make a copy with specific edits. So you can set an AP specific memory card like in the example above. (You don't need to add PINE settings)
For normal setup if you haven't already. Check [GitHub](<https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/blob/main/docs/setup_en.md>), and [Discord](<https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1325015730218860554/1371288772548755466>).
### Start Simply run a `.aprac2` file with Archipelago. (Recommended to set to open `.aprac2` files with Archipelago by default) Or run the client, and use the `/start` command, and then select your desired `.aprac2` file. (Or drag `.aprac2` into the client) Note: You can rename `aprac2` and patched `iso` but they have to have the same name or it'll make another `iso`. (Same way you have to keep `.apsave`, and their `.zip` name the same if hosting locally)
Changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/v0.6.4.1-PINE-pre...v0.6.4.2?w=1> Full changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/main...v0.6.4.2?w=1>

entry.title: Gauntlet Legends v2.1.3
entry.content:
REQUIRED: Archipelago -> https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases REQUIRED: Retroarch -> https://www.retroarch.com/?page=platforms Drag and drop gl.apworld into your custom_worlds folder in your Archipelago install.
You can launch the client from ArchipelagoLauncher, just click on the Gauntlet Legends Client button.
To patch the game, you are required to have a US copy of Gauntlet Legends for the N64. Either drag the patch onto ArchipelagoLauncher.exe or Right click the patch and 'Open With...' ArchipelagoLauncher.exe The host does not need to have a copy of the game to generate.
RETROARCH IS REQUIRED IT IS THE ONLY EMULATOR THAT WILL WORK.
IN RETROARCH: You must go to Settings->User Interface and turn the Show Advanced Settings ON. Then in Settings-> Network, you must also turn ON Network Commands. Leave the port the default.
Make sure you use Mupen64-plus-next in Retroarch as your core.
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jamesbrq/GauntletLegendsAP/compare/v2.1.2...main

entry.title: Dead Rising Deluxe Remaster v0.2.3
entry.content: Added shutters being opened in Entrance Plaza once you talk to Jessie in the Service Hallway Removed Per-Slot random in generation.

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Spirit Tracks v0.7.0
entry.content:
The full game Archipelago release for The Legend of Zelda: Spirit Tracks
- [Setup guide and useful info](https://github.com/DayKat/spirit-tracks/blob/main/worlds/tloz_st/docs/setup.md)
HOTFIX #28: - Fixed compatibility issue with AP versions older than 0.6.7
## New Areas - Ocean Realm by @TriforceDrummer - Fire Realm - Sand Realm - Upper Tower of Spirits
## New Randomized Things - Minigames - Passenger Quests - Cargo Quests - Tears of Light - Shops - Stamps - Boss Keys - Keyrings - Restoration Duets - Custom Combined Rail Items
## New option-y Features - Tower of Spirits unlock options - Death Link (but the train doesn't die yet) - Mcguffin hunt goal, themed as Compass of Light Shards - Train cosmetic options - Treasure farming options - Dungeon pool plando - Cannonless train logic
## Other New Features - Model swaps! Items you find will now show as the correct items. Thanks to @NepetaIsCute for figuring it out! - also added models for most zelda games in AP. Some are closer matches than others. - Tower of Spirits Section Shuffle. - A warp to start option with the client command `/warp_to_start` - Client command `/goal` that shows your current goal progress - Lots of tweaks and fixes

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content: ## Bug Fixes: - Fixed generation issue with tip of the spearhead and escape from bleakrock zone quests causing generation errors

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content: ## Bug Fixes - Fixed bug where mod would check access based on Glenumbra instead of the current zone, leading to zones that are never locked giving a locked zone warning

entry.title: Garfield Kart - Furious Racing v0.5.0b0
entry.content:
Requires [mod v0.5.x](https://github.com/robotzurg/GarfieldKartAPMod/releases/latest)
# What's New? - Kart and Character stats randomizer! Randomize the stats of every kart and character, with options to have good, bad, or medium stats on average. - Lap Count is now split into Lap Count (2-10) and Single Lap Mode (1). Single lap is significantly harder, this should make it harder to accidentally mess yourself up - Lap sanity! Gives out a check every time you finish a lap in first place. - Puzzle Pieces are no longer named , now they're all just called "Puzzle Piece". - New option, Item Mania. CPUs always receive 3 copies of an item if possible. Because we want you to suffer. - Fixed puzzle pieces being marked as progression items even when they're not required for the goal. - Fixed grand prix races not sending individual race checks.
Full Changelog: https://github.com/FeluciaPS/Archipelago/compare/0.4.4-beta...v0.5.0-beta

entry.title: Pokemon Stadium v0.5.1
entry.content: Fixes a bug that was forcing the cursor back to the first Cup tier.

entry.title: Factorio: Space Age v1.1.2
entry.content:
# Factorio: Space Age apworld v2.0.0
Lots of major new changes! Please report bugs and/or balance nightmares. There are a lot of combinations of cool options and I may have overlooked something.
### Gameplay changes
Default options are now a significantly accelerated experience relative to vanilla.
* Added `quick_start` option, enabled by default, that gives personal construction bots and a chunk of basic resources at the start. * Added `skip_starting_trigger_techs` option, disabled by default, that starts with electronics, steam-power, etc. unlocked from the start without needing to do the crafting to trigger them. Free samples are given for skipped trigger techs. * Added `starting_planet` option, disabled by default, that integrates with CodeGreen's Any Planet Start mod: https://mods.factorio.com/mod/any-planet-start . Interacts with `skip_starting_trigger_techs` and `free_samples` in fun ways. * Added `space_technology_level` option to enable space flight with early or mid game technology, effectively downgrading all the ingredients for rocket silo, space platform, thruster, etc. to more primitive items. Puts the Space in Factorio: Space Age sooner rather than near the end of the game. (This could someday be obsoleted by recipe randomization.) * Added `progressive_technologies: large_groups` option, enabled by default, which puts critical technologies, such as advanced circuit, early in large progressive chains with non-critical bonuses later in the chains. This makes it less likely to get stuck waiting for someone to find a specific item. Details here: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/data/ap_data.py * You can Ctrl+F search for vanilla technologies in the technology GUI, and the description tells you which progressive chain it's a part of, if any. (The technologies are impossible to queue up; they only serve to give information to the player about how to !hint for them.) * `goal: any_other_planet_science` is now the default goal, and creates victory technologies to research instead of the mod reacting to researching anything that matches the condition. Includes low-effort art I drew of a trophy. Fixes #5. * `goal: space_platform` replaced by `goal: space_science`, which requires researching a victory technology with 4 science packs including space science (red, green, blue, white). * Managing enemies on Vulcanus and Gleba is now in logic, which is particularly important when starting on those planets. See options `demolisher_killers`, `pentapod_killers`, `vulcanus_rocks`, and `gleba_coal` for more details. Still no logic for Nauvis enemies. * Added `tech_cost_max_count`, default 200, which limits the cost of technology research. Additionally `tech_cost_divisor` defaults to 4 now, so all research objectives are dramatically cheaper by default.
Minor adjustments:
* Energy Link is now enabled by default and the recipe is unlocked by a multiworld item. * Furnaces, electric poles, and `military` through `military-4` recipe technologies are no longer progressive (with `progressive_technologies: only_related`). Getting the recipes out of order is interesting in a randomizer, because there are almost no crafting dependencies between them. * `rocket-silo` research enables ghost entities on death (which normally requires `construction-robotics`). Fixes https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/issues/9 .
### Breaking changes to options
These changes may require updates to your player yaml configuration from v1.
* `progressive_technologies` has been completely overhauled. The old `bonuses` option is gone. The old `recipes` option is very similar to `only_related`. The default has changed to the new `large_groups` option. * `goal: space_platform` removed. Try `goal: space_science` instead. * `shuffle_final_technology` is now merged into `goal` with the addition of `aquilo_orbit_10_science` and `solar_system_edge_11_science`. * The speedups `rocket_parts_per_rocket` and `ingredients_per_space_platform_foundation` are removed because they're now included in `space_technology_level`. * `require_electric_furnace` has been renamed to `require_self_sufficient_space_platform`. * `automation` (the first assembling machine technology) is no longer part of any progressive chain because it is never randomized. * With some `progressive_technologies` settings, several progressive pseudo item names have been simplified to remove the `progressive-` prefix. E.g. `progressive-steel-plate-productivity` is now just called `steel-plate-productivity`, and `worker-robot-speed-1` through `worker-robot-speed-7` are part of a progressive group called simply `worker-robot-speed`. The rough generalization is that recipe unlock chains still say `progressive-` but bonus unlock chains don't. * With `progressive_technologies: large_groups`, if you want to give yourself levels of an infinite tech that's part of a progressive group, name the last item in the chain rather than the chain itself. e.g. `!getitem worker-robot-speed-7` or `start_inventory_from_pool: {mining-productivity-3: 5}`.
### Internal changes
* Overhauled the logic pipeline to support configurable progressive groups, swappable recipes, and other hypothetical future flexibility. The data pipeline starts with Factorio's "prototype" data instead of "runtime" data, and we ship a pruned-down json file instead of generated python code. This change should make this apworld more friendly to contributors by being less confusing/clever/innovative/messy/etc. We do lose the git-controlled representation of the logic graph, which is a little disappointing, but necessary to make it more flexible. * Fixed subtle bug with `on_entity_died` event handler clobbering found by @CosmicWolf. No observable change for the player. TODO: investigate using `on_player_died` instead. * Fixed `/collect` on your own world printing `Unknown Item` warnings related to infinite technologies. * Migrated the data exporter into this repo. Previously located here: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/FactorioInformationExtractor * The logic graph optimizer does more inlining, which results in fewer pedantic steps in the spoiler log for automating and accessing individual items. This could potentially influence progression balancing, but I really don't know if that's how the fill algorithm works.
download: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/releases . general docs: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/en_Factorio_Space_Age.md . setup guide: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/setup_en.md . source code: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/tree/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age .

entry.title: Factorio: Space Age v1.1.2
entry.content:
# Factorio: Space Age apworld v2.0.0
Lots of major new changes! Please report bugs and/or balance nightmares. There are a lot of combinations of cool options and I may have overlooked something.
### Gameplay changes
Default options are now a significantly accelerated experience relative to vanilla.
* Added `quick_start` option, enabled by default, that gives personal construction bots and a chunk of basic resources at the start. * Added `skip_starting_trigger_techs` option, disabled by default, that starts with electronics, steam-power, etc. unlocked from the start without needing to do the crafting to trigger them. Free samples are given for skipped trigger techs. * Added `starting_planet` option, disabled by default, that integrates with CodeGreen's Any Planet Start mod: https://mods.factorio.com/mod/any-planet-start . Interacts with `skip_starting_trigger_techs` and `free_samples` in fun ways. * Added `space_technology_level` option to enable space flight with early or mid game technology, effectively downgrading all the ingredients for rocket silo, space platform, thruster, etc. to more primitive items. Puts the Space in Factorio: Space Age sooner rather than near the end of the game. (This could someday be obsoleted by recipe randomization.) * Added `progressive_technologies: large_groups` option, enabled by default, which puts critical technologies, such as advanced circuit, early in large progressive chains with non-critical bonuses later in the chains. This makes it less likely to get stuck waiting for someone to find a specific item. Details here: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/data/ap_data.py * You can Ctrl+F search for vanilla technologies in the technology GUI, and the description tells you which progressive chain it's a part of, if any. (The technologies are impossible to queue up; they only serve to give information to the player about how to !hint for them.) * `goal: any_other_planet_science` is now the default goal, and creates victory technologies to research instead of the mod reacting to researching anything that matches the condition. Includes low-effort art I drew of a trophy. Fixes #5. * `goal: space_platform` replaced by `goal: space_science`, which requires researching a victory technology with 4 science packs including space science (red, green, blue, white). * Managing enemies on Vulcanus and Gleba is now in logic, which is particularly important when starting on those planets. See options `demolisher_killers`, `pentapod_killers`, `vulcanus_rocks`, and `gleba_coal` for more details. Still no logic for Nauvis enemies. * Added `tech_cost_max_count`, default 200, which limits the cost of technology research. Additionally `tech_cost_divisor` defaults to 4 now, so all research objectives are dramatically cheaper by default.
Minor adjustments:
* Energy Link is now enabled by default and the recipe is unlocked by a multiworld item. * Furnaces, electric poles, and `military` through `military-4` recipe technologies are no longer progressive (with `progressive_technologies: only_related`). Getting the recipes out of order is interesting in a randomizer, because there are almost no crafting dependencies between them. * `rocket-silo` research enables ghost entities on death (which normally requires `construction-robotics`). Fixes https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/issues/9 .
### Breaking changes to options
These changes may require updates to your player yaml configuration from v1.
* `progressive_technologies` has been completely overhauled. The old `bonuses` option is gone. The old `recipes` option is very similar to `only_related`. The default has changed to the new `large_groups` option. * `goal: space_platform` removed. Try `goal: space_science` instead. * `shuffle_final_technology` is now merged into `goal` with the addition of `aquilo_orbit_10_science` and `solar_system_edge_11_science`. * The speedups `rocket_parts_per_rocket` and `ingredients_per_space_platform_foundation` are removed because they're now included in `space_technology_level`. * `require_electric_furnace` has been renamed to `require_self_sufficient_space_platform`. * `automation` (the first assembling machine technology) is no longer part of any progressive chain because it is never randomized. * With some `progressive_technologies` settings, several progressive pseudo item names have been simplified to remove the `progressive-` prefix. E.g. `progressive-steel-plate-productivity` is now just called `steel-plate-productivity`, and `worker-robot-speed-1` through `worker-robot-speed-7` are part of a progressive group called simply `worker-robot-speed`. The rough generalization is that recipe unlock chains still say `progressive-` but bonus unlock chains don't. * With `progressive_technologies: large_groups`, if you want to give yourself levels of an infinite tech that's part of a progressive group, name the last item in the chain rather than the chain itself. e.g. `!getitem worker-robot-speed-7` or `start_inventory_from_pool: {mining-productivity-3: 5}`.
### Internal changes
* Overhauled the logic pipeline to support configurable progressive groups, swappable recipes, and other hypothetical future flexibility. The data pipeline starts with Factorio's "prototype" data instead of "runtime" data, and we ship a pruned-down json file instead of generated python code. This change should make this apworld more friendly to contributors by being less confusing/clever/innovative/messy/etc. We do lose the git-controlled representation of the logic graph, which is a little disappointing, but necessary to make it more flexible. * Fixed subtle bug with `on_entity_died` event handler clobbering found by @CosmicWolf. No observable change for the player. TODO: investigate using `on_player_died` instead. * Fixed `/collect` on your own world printing `Unknown Item` warnings related to infinite technologies. * Migrated the data exporter into this repo. Previously located here: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/FactorioInformationExtractor * The logic graph optimizer does more inlining, which results in fewer pedantic steps in the spoiler log for automating and accessing individual items. This could potentially influence progression balancing, but I really don't know if that's how the fill algorithm works.
download: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/releases . general docs: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/en_Factorio_Space_Age.md . setup guide: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/setup_en.md . source code: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/tree/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age .

entry.title: Muse Dash v1.5.30
entry.content:
# Changes - Adds support for Wuthering Waves Pioneer Podcast and Ay-Aye Horse - Fixed Master Bancho's Sushi Class not showing up.
# Installation ### Melonloader *Melonloader is required to play with this mod. However reinstalling it is not required on update.*
1. Download MelonLoader.Installer.exe from https://github.com/LavaGang/MelonLoader/releases/latest and run it. 2. Choose the automated tab, click the select button and browse to `MuseDash.exe`. - You can find the folder in steam by finding the game in your library, right clicking it and choosing *Manage→Browse Local Files*. - If you click the bar at the top telling you your current folder, this will give you a path you can copy. If you paste that into the window popped up by **MelonLoader**, it will automatically go to the same folder. 3. The launcher will automatically default to the latest version. - If the latest version is not working: **Untick Latest and select 0.6.6.** 4. Click install. Once finished run the game once.
### Mod Install
1.Download `ArchipelagoMuseDash.zip`. 2. Head to the Muse Dash folder and open up `/mods/`. 3. Extract the contents of the .zip file into mods. - Ensure that `ArchipelagoMuseDash.dll` and `Archipelago.MultiClient.Net.dll` are under `/mods/` and not within a sub folder. 4. Run the game. If a new button appears on the start screen the mod was installed correctly.

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Spirit Tracks v0.7.1
entry.content: Works with seeds generated in v0.7.0 [Setup guide and useful info](https://github.com/DayKat/spirit-tracks/blob/main/worlds/tloz_st/docs/setup.md) - fixed bug where your first rabbit caught would crash if caught the first time you entered the overworld - fixed bug where picking up the boss key in ToS 10F would crash due to faulty keyrings - tweaked some yaml wording - prevented Wadatsumi from being in pirate minigame while playing for the gorons - removed HC and Quiver from pirate hideout rewards for more minigame settings where they don't belong - fixed UT randomizing the bypass for ToS 5 - fixed train hiding in garage after getting Niko Stamp Book location

entry.title: Factorio: Space Age v2.0.1
entry.content:
# Factorio: Space Age apworld v2.0.1
* Fix corrupted apworld missing critical files. * Actually bump the version number in the apworld and mod.
download: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/releases . general docs: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/en_Factorio_Space_Age.md . setup guide: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/blob/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age/docs/setup_en.md . source code: https://github.com/thejoshwolfe/Archipelago/tree/space-age/worlds/factorio_space_age .

entry.title: Links Awakening DX Beta v13.4.1
entry.content: - Fix `--no-magpie` flag in client - Fix `improved_additional` warps setting - Fix follower setting

entry.title: Ratchet & Clank 2 v0.6.4.3
entry.content:
Testing Dynamic PINE ports for multi PCSX2 instance support. Also adds auto connection functionally to the client using patch data, and added the manifest json. Based on: https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/releases/tag/v0.6.4 v0.6.4.2: Now also has UT integrated, and yaml-less support. Works as long as UT is installed. Also fixed some first person logic issues. Planned: More UT features. "Glitch" logic. Internal or external map. Auto map tab switching. Community suggested logic changes. v0.6.4.3 Actually made UT work, and added world version to slot data so can see if client isn't matching multiworld.
### Setup You need every RaC2 option in your `host.yaml` setup. That means: `iso_file` to a valid vanilla iso in your Archipelago directory (should be already from normal setup) `iso_start` to your PCSX2 exe (Make sure to use `/`) `game_ini` to a game settings file in your PCSX2's gamesettings folder. (Usually `%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini`)
Example: ```yaml rac2_options: # File name of the Ratchet & Clank 2 ISO iso_file: "Ratchet & Clank - Going Commando (USA) (v1.01).iso" # Set this false to never autostart an iso (such as after patching), # Set it to true to have the operating system default program open the iso # Alternatively, set it to a path to a program to open the .iso file with (like PCSX2) iso_start: "C:/Emulators/(PS2) PCSX2/pcsx2-qt.exe" # Set to file path to an existing PCSX2 game setting INI file to have the patcher # create an appropriately named INI with the rest of the patch output. This can be used to # allow you to use you own custom PCSX2 setting with patched ISO. game_ini: "%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini" ```
Game settings ini example: ```ini [EmuCore] EnablePINE = true PINESlot = 28011
[Achievements] Enabled = false
[MemoryCards] Slot1_Enable = true Slot2_Enable = false Slot1_Filename = APgames.ps2 ``` Recommended you use your settings for vanilla RaC2. But you can make a copy with specific edits. So you can set an AP specific memory card like in the example above. (You don't need to add PINE settings)
For normal setup if you haven't already. Check [GitHub](<https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/blob/main/docs/setup_en.md>), and [Discord](<https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1325015730218860554/1371288772548755466>).
### Start Simply run a `.aprac2` file with Archipelago. (Recommended to set to open `.aprac2` files with Archipelago by default) Or run the client, and use the `/start` command, and then select your desired `.aprac2` file. (Or drag `.aprac2` into the client) Note: You can rename `aprac2` and patched `iso` but they have to have the same name or it'll make another `iso`. (Same way you have to keep `.apsave`, and their `.zip` name the same if hosting locally)
Changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/v0.6.4.2...v0.6.4.3?w=1> Full changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/main...v0.6.4.3?w=1>

entry.title: Elder Scrolls Online v0.3.0
entry.content: ## Bug Fixes: - Fixed issue with unlocked delves being treated as locked

entry.title: Mega Man Zero 3 v0.2.2
entry.content:
Sub Tanks have been implemented!
# Changes - Sub Tanks have been implemented as location checks and randomized items. - This update should have performance increases. It turns out you can read multiple slots of memory in one Bizhawk read/write statement. Very cool. - Fixed a bug where having multiple clients running on the same computer could cause the two games to incorrectly share inventory.

entry.title: Kingdom Hearts Dream Drop Distance v0.0.6
entry.content:
# Kingdom Hearts Dream Drop Distance Archipelago 0.0.6
*This version has **breaking** changes. Please do not update to this version if you have a run ongoing.* ### APWorld Changes - Fixed several logic issues in Traverse Town - Made socket non-blocking for better Linux compatibility - Defeating Ansem II now awards 3 checks (one for each boss in the gauntlet)
### AP Companion Changes - New AP Item Icon (Thanks Halrondo!) ***NEW QOL*** - Added a Report that details your Go Mode conditions and whether you have achieved them (can be found in Reports -> Glossaries) - All save points in a world will unlock after clearing that world - Available Flowmotion is now accurately reflected in action command inventory - A world item for TT2 now appears in inventory when it's accessible - Active Spirits are now affected by the EXP Multiplier - Spirit Rank now randomized when auto-crafting - Obtained Exp now restored after death *** BUG FIXES*** - Reworked some under-the-hood stuff for stability and performance (this involves making certain checks much less frequent, so please report if you encounter any oddities!) - Reworked EXP Multiplier to prevent early level up checks - Removed purgatory (maybe finally?) - Flick Rush option no longer sends the player to Traverse Town - Fixed a bug where certain rooms became inaccessible - Fixed a bug where action commands could not be equipped - Fixed an issue where spirits sent several level up prompts - Other minor fixes

entry.title: Warioworld v0.1.9.post3
entry.content: # Warioworld Archipelago v0.1.9 ### Additions > Added RingLink (all coins will count as 1 for balance purposes, you still lose the same amount of coins however) > Added SFX functionality for RingLink > Added PAL support (the Client will also apply a different patch depending on PAL or NTSC) # Fixes > Fixed going into/out of a Sublevel sending a DeathLink to other players > Fixed DeathLink not being received by Wario (no more favourable exchange rates for you sir!) > Fixed miscellaneous checks being given to you when they shouldn't > Fixed Chests randomly spawning without you hitting the Button > Fixed the logic for Clown-a-Round being incorrect (leading to the game thinking you had access when you did not) > Closing the game before the Client can sometimes send random checks and crash the Client > Fixed Client disconnecting when your Coin count goes below 0 # Known Bugs > The most recent item will be given to you again if it is a Filler/Trap item on reloading the savefile (this can softlock you if you have been given the Death Trap last, being sent another item will fix this) > Some checks need to be collected twice to register (this is random and inconsistent, even across savestates)

entry.title: Pokemon Black and White v0.3.27
entry.content:
# Changelog
- Fixed `Experience Multiplier` not automatically being set to the value specified in the yaml - Fixed `All Pokémon Seen` not being applied to the save file - Tried fixing a rare bug regarding the PopTracker pack and data storage - Removed `any_badge`/`any_tm_hm` from `Shuffle Badge Rewards`/`Shuffle TM Rewards` - Old yamls with these choices will instead interpret them as `shuffled` - Removed `Prioritize key item locations` from `Modify Logic` - Old yamls with this modifier will just ignore it
### Is this version compatible with older multiworld slots and save files? Yes, back to 0.3.0, except the pre-release 0.3.99(9) However, tracking worlds generated before 0.3.27 might show incorrect Dexsanity logic in UT
### Notes regarding the `pokemon_bw_without_maps.apworld` file That version does not contain the submaps for UT auto-tabbing, other than that it's identical to the full apworld. To install it, you have to either double klick it to install or manually rename it to `pokemon_bw.apworld`. It's only a fraction of the size of the full apworld and intended for people who don't use UT, which includes multiworld hosts.

entry.title: YARG v0.0.11
entry.content:
# Use Client Version 0.0.5. No Client Update Required!
New: - Added YARN Request Pack 1
## What's Changed * Add instrument shuffle details to readme by @energymaster22 in https://github.com/energymaster22/YARGArchipelago/pull/20 * added YARN Request Pack 1 by @energymaster22 in https://github.com/energymaster22/YARGArchipelago/pull/22
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/energymaster22/YARGArchipelago/compare/0.0.10...0.0.11

entry.title: Devil May Cry 3 v0.5.0
entry.content:
- Now uses the new and improved rewrite for archipelago_rs (Big kudos to @nex3) - Universal Tracker support, UT can work without the yaml. This also makes it so UT is aware of the random mission order - New Hint Game mode (This will appear as a separate client in the AP Launcher). Do Bloody Palace floors to generate hints! Number of floors needed per hint can be adjusted in the `host.yaml` - Purchasing blue/purple orbs can be checks. (Purchasing gun levels and skills checks will most likely come in a later update) - Red Orb Filler has been added (1000/2500/5000) to alleviate some grinding - Say goodbye to the bank system. Item Syncing has been re-written and is now based off of the currently loaded save slot - Skills are now properly given mid mission (and overall behave better) - Crystal skull location now works, oops - Re-organized where saves and sync data are. Files are now under `game_install/archipelago/(seed_{slot_name})/`
APWorld Specific: - SS Rank Checks can be entirely removed - There are a whole lot less generation failures now! - Gun upgrades are now classified as useful.

entry.title: Pokemon Black and White v0.3.28
entry.content:
# Changelog
- Fixed generation failures resulting from the last update
### Is this version compatible with older multiworld slots and save files? Yes, back to 0.3.0, except the pre-release 0.3.99(9) However, tracking worlds generated before 0.3.27 might show incorrect Dexsanity logic in UT
### Notes regarding the `pokemon_bw_without_maps.apworld` file That version does not contain the submaps for UT auto-tabbing, other than that it's identical to the full apworld. To install it, you have to either double klick it to install or manually rename it to `pokemon_bw.apworld`. It's only a fraction of the size of the full apworld and intended for people who don't use UT, which includes multiworld hosts.

entry.title: Autopelago v0.11.0
entry.content:
# https://autopelago.app
The game client has been completely rewritten from scratch. It runs in the browser now.
The .apworld now has a manifest, but has otherwise not changed since 0.10.3.
# Changes since 0.11.14
1. Fix an unnecessary error when connecting to multiworlds generated from APWorld files predating April 1, 2025. Ongoing support for multiworlds generated from old 0.x APWorld files is not guaranteed, but if the only thing I need to do is slightly tweak a version number check, then that's no big deal.
Everything from the original native version *should* be present in this one. If not, then that's a bug. Please report it.
# Other changes since 0.10.4
1. You no longer need to run any native executable files. It's a web app now, so just go to that URL. - If you want to self-host it, it's all static files - this release should have a "dist.zip" asset that you can just drop into any reasonable static web server. - You will **need** to self-host if you want it to work with an Archipelago server that doesn't use HTTPS. 2. You can now launch directly to the game if you know all the magical things to type into the URL fragment. This is mostly provided so you can refresh the page without having to click through the menu again. 3. As is web tradition, most errors that happen when the game is running will not automatically take you out of the game screen. Instead, they will just create banners along the top that you have to click to dismiss. Hopefully, this will never happen to you (of course, it happened to me constantly while developing this). 4. Not only is this a web app (and so it finally runs on Mac and Android), but it's a **progressive** web app. This means that you can "install" it as a native app on Android, even. 5. Interval between "when STILL blocked" messages is now configurable (though it's still got a minimum of 15 minutes so that you don't spam everyone). 6. There's now a separate "Connecting..." screen between the settings page and the main game screen, instead of showing a version of the game screen instantly and then modifying it once you're finally connected. 7. When you get disconnected for any reason (other than clicking the button to go back to the menu), you now get spat back to the same "Connecting..." screen (with a different message) and it will try to reconnect you automatically, at least once every 30 seconds. 8. The game version is now visible in the title bar and on the game screen. This is more relevant here because browsers like to cache way too much stuff. 9. Somewhat redesigned the layout of the game screen to be a bit clearer. Instead of shorthand "RAT" / "NOM" / etc., we have a "RATS: ##" expander, and then all the active buffs / debuffs are visible with their full names under it. This takes up a lot of screen space, so you can collapse it to just show the rat count. 10. The left panel can also shrink MUCH more than before. 11. The position of the grid separator is now saved between runs. 12. If your slot has an alias, we now show that (without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always just showing the official slot name. 13. Chat commands can now be addressed to the rat's alias (with or without the parenthetical slot name) instead of always having to be its slot name, provided that nobody else in the room has picked the same alias. 14. Added a simple chat tab to just send / receive messages in the room, so that you don't HAVE to flip back and forth. It's not quite as fancy as the standard text client, but it should be good enough. 15. Elements that you can interact with all support tab-focusing, and the tooltips as well. 16. When a lot of things happen all at once that the game hasn't animated yet, the app will try to play back everything that happened. This was important when moving to a web app, because browsers don't render things when the tab is in the background or minimized, so when you come back, a LOT of things could have changed. In fact, when LOTS of things changed, it speeds up the playback up to 5x so that you can "catch up" to the current state more quickly. 17. When clicking on an item in the panel to request a hint, the confirmation box now shows up right next to it instead of in the middle of the screen. 18. The "u win." screen now just covers a *part* of the map instead of taking you all the way out. 19. Fix an oversight where the self-host bundle was still expecting to run on `https://autopelago.app` 20. Add a discriminator to the beginning of the title for the benefit of those with multiple tabs 21. #129: Bring back the ability to show the path to the target by clicking on the rat. 22. Fix a minor issue where the rat would face its ultimate target instead of the next location along the route to it. 23. Fix an issue where the rat would go to the nearest user-requested location instead of going in order of when they were requested. 24. (After the failed v0.11.2) probably fix a calculation error in the "show path" calculation? 25. #130: Bring back the ability to personalize your rat. 26. Replace Pixi.js with native elements, which should unblock those getting the error "`CanvasRenderer` is not yet implemented". This also probably brings with it a mixed bag of performance penalties and performance improvements. 27. Fix several ways in which the page could look self-inconsistent while the rat is still catching up to where the server is. 28. Correct the size of the "Personalize Your Rat!" button on the main page for non-Firefox browsers. 29. Remove a brief flicker of the "Personalize Your Rat!" window's header text. It might still sometimes shift, but generally shouldn't have any frames where the text goes outside the window like it did. 30. Add tooltips for the filler squares to show their names, exactly the same as tooltips for landmarks. 31. Add information about known hints onto these tooltips. 32. Move some quest markers out of the way so that they no longer overlap filler squares. 33. Shrink landmark images so that they don't crowd their filler squares. 34. Extra-shrink the hitboxes around the quest markers to make their nearby filler squares easier to get to. 35. Add the ability to "hyper-focus" a location by clicking on it (or pressing Enter on it if you are tabbing through with your keyboard). This "hyper-focus" can only be adjusted by interacting with it in the browser (no chat commands supported, by design) for multiple reasons. 36. #134: Fix an issue where the rat's focused location(s) would get cleared — even if the location wasn't checked — if it happened to land on the location without any actions remaining to make an attempt at checking it. 37. Fix an oversight from 0.11.5 where the animation checkboxes did nothing. - In addition to restoring the original few things that these checkboxes control: 1. Transitions on Food/Energy/Luck bars will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 2. Tile examples in the tooltips and the "u win." dialog will be animated (or not) along with the tiles, synchronized to the same frame. 3. The dashed line showing the path from the rat to its target location will be animated (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 4. The rat token will animate its movement from one location to the next (or not) according to the player animation checkbox. 5. On systems where the user has indicated a [preference for "reduced motion"](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@media/prefers-reduced-motion#user_preferences), the two checkboxes will be unchecked by default. 38. #143: Stop automatically reconnecting after getting disconnected. This has been resurrecting rooms that had previously timed out, which is rude. Some version of this MAY come back in the future, if I can find a way to detect when it would be OK. 39. #142: The dashed path to the rat's current target will no longer get misaligned and shrunk on games that don't go all the way to Snakes on a Planet. 40. #138: The sidebar now has an extra spot at the beginning that shows the number of all rats. This lets you do the same stuff that the other icons in this panel let you do: click to hint for any rat (i.e., any item in the "Rats" item group) and hover over it to see a tooltip with all the hinted rat locations. 41. Because I kept getting annoyed while testing the previous one, tooltips now stay around when the cursor goes inside them instead of needing the cursor to stay inside the original trigger box. This means that you can now highlight text from these tooltips with your mouse and copy-paste. 42. I might have fixed a bug where sometimes the rat wouldn't send its chat messages immediately, but would wait for some arbitrary event to happen in the future. I have no idea why I wrote it the way I did at first... 43. Whenever we see `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` in any message from your YAML file, we'll replace that with the current alias of a random player. I tried to make this as useful and sensible as I could: - If you are the only player (on your team), it will always just pick you. Otherwise, it will never pick you, but instead: - If any other players (on your team) are connected and haven't completed their goal yet, then it will pick a random one of them. Otherwise, it will pick a random other player (on your team). - If there are multiple `{RANDOM_PLAYER}` placeholders in a single message, then we'll try to pick as many **different** players as possible (in random order still), only repeating a player if there are more placeholders than the number of players allowed by the above rules. To be precise, we sample randomly without replacement from the list until the list is empty, then we refill it and repeat the process until all placeholders have been filled. 44. Fix an oversight that caused messages to get repeatedly processed over and over again. This was most visible when telling the rat to do something: it would go into an infinite loop of acknowledging the request forever. 45. Fix an oversight where a horizontal scroll bar could appear under normal conditions just because of how close certain locations are to the edge. 46. Set the base URL to allow self-hosting it somewhere other than the site root (e.g., if I were to make subpaths like `https://old.autopelago.app/0.11.13` to run that older version). 47. Start looking for the bits that version 1.0.0 of the APWorld will (later!) start to send us to support more flavor items with only Archipelago-side updates. 48. For games with passwords, start encrypting what shows up in the URL bar. There's a lot more to it than that sounds: the encrypted password uses `&W=EncryptedPassword`, but you can still manually plug in `&w=OriginalPassword`. The encryption is there so that you can reload / bookmark the page and still connect to the game just fine, as long as you do so within the same browser profile (i.e., you can't paste your URL from Firefox to Chrome or to someone else's computer, the key is only stored locally).

entry.title: Ratchet & Clank 2 v0.6.4.4
entry.content:
Testing Dynamic PINE ports for multi PCSX2 instance support. Also adds auto connection functionally to the client using patch data, and added the manifest json. Based on: https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/releases/tag/v0.6.4 v0.6.4.4: Now has UT integrated, and yaml-less support. Works as long as UT is installed. Also fixed some first person logic issues. Thanks to @AmondoVagyok Also added world version to slot data so can see if client isn't matching multiworld gen. Planned: More UT features. "Glitch" logic. Internal or external map. Auto map tab switching. Community suggested logic changes.
### Setup You need every RaC2 option in your `host.yaml` setup. That means: `iso_file` to a valid vanilla iso in your Archipelago directory (should be already from normal setup) `iso_start` to your PCSX2 exe (Make sure to use `/`) `game_ini` to a game settings file in your PCSX2's gamesettings folder. (Usually `%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini`)
Example: ```yaml rac2_options: # File name of the Ratchet & Clank 2 ISO iso_file: "Ratchet & Clank - Going Commando (USA) (v1.01).iso" # Set this false to never autostart an iso (such as after patching), # Set it to true to have the operating system default program open the iso # Alternatively, set it to a path to a program to open the .iso file with (like PCSX2) iso_start: "C:/Emulators/(PS2) PCSX2/pcsx2-qt.exe" # Set to file path to an existing PCSX2 game setting INI file to have the patcher # create an appropriately named INI with the rest of the patch output. This can be used to # allow you to use you own custom PCSX2 setting with patched ISO. game_ini: "%USERPROFILE%/Documents/PCSX2/gamesettings/SCUS-97268_38996035.ini" ```
Game settings ini example: ```ini [EmuCore] EnablePINE = true PINESlot = 28011
[Achievements] Enabled = false
[MemoryCards] Slot1_Enable = true Slot2_Enable = false Slot1_Filename = APgames.ps2 ``` Recommended you use your settings for vanilla RaC2. But you can make a copy with specific edits. So you can set an AP specific memory card like in the example above. (You don't need to add PINE settings)
For normal setup if you haven't already. Check [GitHub](<https://github.com/evilwb/APRac2/blob/main/docs/setup_en.md>), and [Discord](<https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1325015730218860554/1371288772548755466>).
### Start Simply run a `.aprac2` file with Archipelago. (Recommended to set to open `.aprac2` files with Archipelago by default) Or run the client, and use the `/start` command, and then select your desired `.aprac2` file. (Or drag `.aprac2` into the client) Note: You can rename `aprac2` and patched `iso` but they have to have the same name or it'll make another `iso`. (Same way you have to keep `.apsave`, and their `.zip` name the same if hosting locally)
Changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/v0.6.4.1-PINE-pre...v0.6.4.4?w=1> Full changes: <https://github.com/jacobmix/APRac2/compare/main...v0.6.4.3?w=1>

entry.title: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Seasons v20.0.0
entry.content:
### This apworld is a beta apworld, only compatible with Archipelago 0.6.7 (in RC as of the release of this release)
## 🗳️ New Options * Puzzle and Minigames rando * When randomized, the games are about as hard as vanilla, with 2 less screens for hide & seek * Owl-related puzzles are not affected * No push block is added * Linked Hero's cave can now be made reachable from a warp in the regular hero's cave
## ⏫ Miscellaneous Improvements * Used up gasha spots are now represented with the dug tile * The game now shows which item is being obtained when the item is from a different world * Generation is faster * Asking for `included essences` placed essences will set the placed essences to the number included
## 🧠 Logic * Cane of somaria is now a in-logic way to kill golden beasts (it's decently hard) * Bomb/bombchu requirement is now more consistent, unless there is a consistent way to refill it * 40 bombs are required to fight * 20 bombs are required to destroy bombable tiles * 20 bombs are required for bombjumps * 20 bombchus are required to fight * 40 bombchus are required to destroy tiles * Jumping the goron pits with just a cape has been upgraded from medium to hard logic * Some anti-softlocks have been removed, which affects some of the logic * Added a logic path to leave d2 * Fixed a logic issue where the d8 spinner chest was reachable without magnet glove * Added a hell logic path from frypolar to d8 spinner chest without magnet glove
## 🛠️ Bugfixes * Made use of pre_output, unit tests now work on OoS * Fixed a text at random being broken each seed * Fixed dimitri clipping and respawning at the closed d4 entrance * Melting the red and blue ores, then leaving the room to come back no longer gives hard ore instead of the normal item

entry.title: KeyWe v1.1.0
entry.content:
- Fix for new game update
[Client App](https://github.com/xMcacutt-Archipelago/KeyWe-AP-Client)
